_source: manually researched from official docs (airtable.com/developers/web/api/oauth-reference and airtable.com/developers/web/guides/oauth-integrations)
_note: Airtable OAuth2 uses PKCE (code_challenge / code_verifier). Refresh tokens expire after 60 days of inactivity. When refreshing, Airtable issues a new refresh token and invalidates the previous one -- always persist the new refresh_token. No dedicated token revocation endpoint appears to be publicly documented. Alternative auth: Personal Access Tokens (PATs) created in the Airtable UI.
